data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260509T221701-aadd5477/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260509T221701-aadd5477/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260509T221701-aadd5477/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260509T221701-aadd5477/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260509T221701-aadd5477/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-128c7f9d">
    <title>agent/lock: instrument bead.Store WithLock wait time + tracker-lock acquisition metrics</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  When parallel-worker drain mode is enabled (multiple ddx work --once
  shells running concurrently), bead.Store.WithLock and the agent-layer
  tracker locks become a contention surface. Today there is no
  observability into lock wait time, lock-hold duration, or contention
  rate — failures manifest as 30s MaxElapsed exhaustion and
  resource_exhausted outcomes, with no telemetry to debug.

  The 2026-05-09 plan-review opus self-review identified this as a
  prerequisite for safely scaling up to N=2..4 parallel workers: without
  per-lock metrics, we cannot tell whether a 10s WithLock wait
  (lock.go:18-27 default) is approaching its budget under load, or
  whether 30s tracker-lock MaxElapsed (tracker_lock.go:51) is being
  exhausted by a long-tail of concurrent acquisitions vs occasional
  hot-path blocking.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/bead/lock.go:18-27 WithLock has no instrumentation; it
    spins at 50ms intervals and waits up to 10s by default. Wait time is
    not measured or emitted.
  - cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock.go:105-183 withTrackerLock /
    withMainGitLock acquire/release path has no instrumentation.
  - There is no operator-facing dashboard, log, or metric for lock
    contention.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add lightweight metrics around three lock surfaces:

  1. bead.Store.WithLock (cli/internal/bead/lock.go:18-27):
     - Record lock-wait duration (time from WithLock entry to lock
       acquisition).
     - Record lock-hold duration (time the callback held the lock).
     - Emit via the existing agentmetrics package (or a new bead-metrics
       channel) keyed by call site.

  2. withTrackerLock / withMainGitLock (cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock.go:105-183):
     - Same wait/hold instrumentation.
     - Additional: record retry-attempt count when DefaultLockRetryPolicy
       is consumed (so we see "needed 3 retries" vs "got it on first
       attempt").

  3. Exit code / stop reason aggregation:
     - When a worker times out at MaxElapsed, capture the lock identity
       and the holder's claimant-PID at point of exhaustion. Today the
       error message is generic.

  Surface format:
     - Existing agentmetrics output (likely .ddx/agent-metrics or
       similar; check cli/internal/agentmetrics for existing API).
     - Or stdout JSON line per lock acquire/release if no agentmetrics
       sink exists.

  Add tests:
     - TestWithLock_RecordsWaitDuration: assert metric is captured for a
       contended lock.
     - TestTrackerLock_RecordsRetryCount: assert retry count metric is
       captured under simulated contention.

NON-SCOPE
  - Fixing lock contention itself (sister bead ddx-1f774edf removes the
    big contributor; this bead is observability for what's left).
  - Building a lock-contention dashboard (separate concern; just emit
    the data).
  - Reducing the 10s/30s budgets (those are correct; the metric tells
    us if they're tight).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf (network-free Land()) drops the big
    contention cause; this bead provides visibility into what remains.
  - Sister bead ddx-c019e48a (chaos for stale-lock recovery) covers
    correctness; this bead covers visibility.
  - Sister bead (just-filed) "agent/chaos: cover concurrent-drain
    landing under shared-main contention" needs this metrics surface to
    write meaningful assertions.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/bead/lock.go WithLock records wait-duration and hold-duration via agentmetrics or equivalent observable channel.
2. cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock.go withTrackerLock / withMainGitLock records wait, hold, and retry-count metrics.
3. When tracker_lock.go exhausts DefaultLockRetryPolicy.MaxElapsed, the resulting error message includes the lock identity and the holder's claimant-PID (not just a generic timeout).
4. TestWithLock_RecordsWaitDuration in cli/internal/bead/lock_test.go (or new test file) asserts the metric is captured for a contended lock.
5. TestTrackerLock_RecordsRetryCount in cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock_test.go (or new test file) asserts retry count metric is captured under simulated contention.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, area:lock, kind:feature, observability, prevention</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="218b6bf7179ddf5b9210a0ec5730b2fc62bb3606" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260509T221701-aadd5477"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Commit succeeded. All 7 ACs are satisfied:

1. **AC#1** — `bead/lock.go` `WithLock` records `Wait` (spin-to-acquire) and `Hold` (fn runtime) via `LockMetricsSink`.
2. **AC#2** — `agent/tracker_lock.go` `withTrackerLockPolicy` records `Wait`, `Hold`, and `Retries` (sleep-and-retry count) via `TrackerLockMetricsSink`.
3. **AC#3** — `lockTimeoutError` now includes the lock directory path (`lock: /path/to/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock`) alongside `owner pid:` in the timeout message.
4. **AC#4** — `TestWithLock_RecordsWaitDuration` in `bead/lock_metrics_test.go` passes.
5. **AC#5** — `TestTrackerLock_RecordsRetryCount` in `agent/tracker_lock_test.go` passes.
6. **AC#6** — `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/...` green.
7. **AC#7** — `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
